concepts.name: natural cubic spline
concepts.description: A cubic spline with the additional constraint that the function is linear beyond the boundary knots, reducing boundary variance.
concepts.quote: A natural cubic spline adds additional constraints, namely that the function is linear beyond the boundary knots. This frees up four degrees of freedom (two constraints each in both boundary regions), which can be spent more profitably by sprinkling more knots in the interior region.

concepts.name: order-M spline
concepts.description: A piecewise-polynomial of order M with continuous derivatives up to order M-2 at the knots.
concepts.quote: More generally, an order-M spline with knots ξj, j = 1, . . . , K is a piecewise-polynomial of order M, and has continuous derivatives up to order M − 2.

concepts.name: cubic spline
concepts.description: An order-4 spline with continuous first and second derivatives at knots, the lowest order where knot discontinuity is invisible to the human eye.
concepts.quote: It is claimed that cubic splines are the lowest-order spline for which the knot-discontinuity is not visible to the human eye.

concepts.name: truncated power basis
concepts.description: A conceptually simple but numerically problematic basis for representing splines using polynomial terms and truncated power functions.
concepts.quote: While the truncated power basis is conceptually simple, it is not too attractive numerically: powers of large numbers can lead to severe rounding problems.

concepts.name: pointwise variance
concepts.description: The variance of a fitted function at a specific point, measuring uncertainty in the fit at that location.
concepts.quote: This can be conveniently summarized in terms of the pointwise variance of spline functions fit by least squares.

concepts.name: backward stepwise deletion
concepts.description: A model selection procedure that iteratively removes terms from a model to minimize an information criterion while preserving group structure.
concepts.quote: We carried out a backward stepwise deletion process, dropping terms from this model while preserving the group structure of each term, rather than dropping one coefficient at a time.

concepts.name: functional modeling
concepts.description: Using splines to reduce rather than increase flexibility by constraining coefficients to vary smoothly as functions.
concepts.quote: In this example we use splines to reduce flexibility rather than increase it; the application comes under the general heading of functional modeling.

concepts.name: contrast functional
concepts.description: A function of coefficients that highlights regions where classes differ, having appreciable values where features discriminate between classes.
concepts.quote: The coefficients compute a contrast functional, and will have appreciable values in regions of frequency where the log-periodograms differ between the two classes.

concepts.name: feature extraction
concepts.description: Preprocessing high-dimensional features through transformation (linear or nonlinear) before applying a learning algorithm.
concepts.quote: Preprocessing of high-dimensional features is a very general and powerful method for improving the performance of a learning algorithm.

concepts.name: filtered features
concepts.description: Transformed features x* derived from raw features x through basis expansion or other preprocessing.
concepts.quote: These filtered versions of the features were then used as inputs into a learning procedure

concepts.name: smoothing spline
concepts.description: A spline that minimizes penalized RSS with a knot at every unique x value, controlling complexity via a roughness penalty rather than knot selection.
concepts.quote: Here we discuss a spline basis method that avoids the knot selection problem completely by using a maximal set of knots. The complexity of the fit is controlled by regularization.

concepts.name: penalized residual sum of squares
concepts.description: An objective function balancing data fit against function roughness, with a smoothing parameter controlling the tradeoff.
concepts.quote: among all functions f(x) with two continuous derivatives, find one that minimizes the penalized residual sum of squares... The first term measures closeness to the data, while the second term penalizes curvature in the function, and λ establishes a tradeoff between the two.

concepts.name: roughness penalty
concepts.description: A penalty term based on the integrated squared second derivative, penalizing curvature in the function.
concepts.quote: the second term penalizes curvature in the function

concepts.name: Sobolev space
concepts.description: An infinite-dimensional function space of functions with bounded derivatives, used as the domain for smoothing spline optimization.
concepts.quote: The criterion (5.9) is defined on an infinite-dimensional function space—in fact, a Sobolev space of functions for which the second term is defined.

concepts.name: generalized ridge regression
concepts.description: Ridge regression with a general penalty matrix rather than identity, arising from smoothing spline formulation.
concepts.quote: The solution is easily seen to be θ̂ = (NᵀN + λΩₙ)⁻¹Nᵀy, a generalized ridge regression.

concepts.name: linear smoother
concepts.description: A fitting method where fitted values are a linear function of responses, characterized by a smoother matrix independent of y.
concepts.quote: A smoothing spline with prechosen λ is an example of a linear smoother (as in linear operator). This is because the estimated parameters in (5.12) are a linear combination of the yᵢ.

concepts.name: hat matrix
concepts.description: The projection operator in ordinary least squares that maps responses to fitted values, an idempotent matrix.
concepts.quote: Here the linear operator Hξ is a projection operator, also known as the hat matrix in statistics.

concepts.name: effective degrees of freedom
concepts.description: The trace of the smoother matrix, providing a unified measure of model complexity across different smoothing methods.
concepts.quote: By analogy we define the effective degrees of freedom... This very useful definition allows us a more intuitive way to parameterize the smoothing spline, and indeed many other smoothers as well, in a consistent fashion.

concepts.name: Reinsch form
concepts.description: An alternative representation Sλ = (I + λK)⁻¹ separating the smoothing parameter from the penalty structure.
concepts.quote: it is convenient to rewrite Sλ in the Reinsch form Sλ = (I + λK)⁻¹, where K does not depend on λ.

concepts.name: projection smoothers
concepts.description: Smoothers with idempotent smoother matrices having eigenvalues of exactly 1 or 0, selecting basis functions without shrinkage.
concepts.quote: a projection matrix such as Hξ above has M eigenvalues equal to 1, and the rest are 0. For this reason smoothing splines are referred to as shrinking smoothers, while regression splines are projection smoothers

concepts.name: Demmler-Reinsch basis
concepts.description: The eigenvector basis of the smoother matrix, providing a representation where the penalty is diagonal.
concepts.quote: One can reparametrize the smoothing spline using the basis vectors uₖ (the Demmler–Reinsch basis).

concepts.name: integrated squared prediction error
concepts.description: Expected prediction error averaged over both training samples and prediction points, combining bias and variance.
concepts.quote: The integrated squared prediction error (EPE) combines both bias and variance in a single summary... Note that this is averaged both over the training sample (giving rise to f̂λ), and the values of the (independently chosen) prediction points (X, Y).

concepts.name: nonparametric logistic regression
concepts.description: Logistic regression with a smooth nonparametric function replacing the linear predictor, fit via penalized log-likelihood.
concepts.quote: The smoothing spline problem (5.9) in Section 5.4 is posed in a regression setting. It is typically straightforward to transfer this technology to other domains. Here we consider logistic regression with a single quantitative input X.

concepts.name: penalized log-likelihood
concepts.description: A log-likelihood criterion augmented with a roughness penalty for fitting smooth nonparametric models.
concepts.quote: We construct the penalized log-likelihood criterion

concepts.name: weighted smoothing spline
concepts.description: A smoothing spline fit with observation-specific weights, arising in iterative algorithms like Newton-Raphson for GLMs.
concepts.quote: Referring back to (5.12) and (5.14), we see that the update fits a weighted smoothing spline to the working response z

concepts.name: working response
concepts.description: An adjusted response variable used in iterative weighted least squares algorithms for fitting GLMs.
concepts.quote: the update fits a weighted smoothing spline to the working response z

concepts.name: tensor product basis
concepts.description: A multidimensional basis formed by taking products of one-dimensional basis functions for each coordinate.
concepts.quote: Then the M₁ × M₂ dimensional tensor product basis defined by... can be used for representing a two-dimensional function

concepts.name: reproducing kernel Hilbert space
concepts.description: A Hilbert space of functions with a reproducing kernel, providing a unifying framework for regularization and spline smoothing.
concepts.quote: Regularization and Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Spaces

concepts.name: wavelet smoothing
concepts.description: A smoothing method using wavelet basis functions, effective for functions with discontinuities and sharp features.
concepts.quote: Wavelet Smoothing

concepts.name: wavelet basis
concepts.description: A basis of wavelet functions for representing signals, allowing multi-resolution analysis.
concepts.quote: Wavelet Bases and the Wavelet Transform

concepts.name: wavelet transform
concepts.description: A transformation decomposing signals into wavelet coefficients at different scales and locations.
concepts.quote: for signal or image recognition a popular approach is to first transform the raw features via a wavelet transform x* = Hᵀx (Section 5.9)

concepts.name: adaptive wavelet filtering
concepts.description: A wavelet smoothing method that adaptively selects coefficients based on data-driven criteria.
concepts.quote: Adaptive Wavelet Filtering

concepts.name: kernel smoother
concepts.description: A nonparametric regression method using kernel-weighted local averages.
concepts.quote: One-Dimensional Kernel Smoothers

concepts.name: local linear regression
concepts.description: A kernel smoother fitting local linear models, automatically correcting boundary bias.
concepts.quote: Local Linear Regression

concepts.name: AIC statistic
concepts.description: Akaike Information Criterion - a model selection criterion balancing fit and complexity, used for term selection in stepwise procedures.
concepts.quote: The AIC statistic (Section 7.5) was used to drop terms, and all the terms remaining in the final model would cause AIC to increase if deleted from the model

concepts.name: B-spline basis
concepts.description: A numerically stable basis for representing splines, allowing efficient computation even with many knots.
concepts.quote: The B-spline basis, described in the Appendix to this chapter, allows for efficient computations even when the number of knots K is large.

concepts.name: boundary knots
concepts.description: The extreme knots defining the domain boundaries where spline behavior can become erratic.
concepts.quote: The polynomials fit beyond the boundary knots behave even more wildly than the corresponding global polynomials in that region.

concepts.name: interior knots
concepts.description: Knots placed within the data range, controlling spline flexibility in regions with data.
concepts.quote: bs(x,df=7) in R generates a basis matrix of cubic-spline functions evaluated at the N observations in x, with the 7−3 = 4 interior knots

concepts.name: generalized cross-validation
concepts.description: A model selection technique related to cross-validation but computationally more efficient, used for choosing smoothing parameters.
concepts.quote: techniques such as K-fold cross-validation, GCV and Cp are all in common use

concepts.name: Cp statistic
concepts.description: Mallows' Cp - a model selection criterion estimating prediction error, commonly used for subset selection.
concepts.quote: techniques such as K-fold cross-validation, GCV and Cp are all in common use

concepts.name: automatic kernel carpentry
concepts.description: The phenomenon where local linear regression automatically modifies the kernel to correct boundary bias exactly to first order.
concepts.quote: Local linear regression automatically modifies the kernel to correct the bias exactly to first order, a phenomenon dubbed as automatic kernel carpentry.

concepts.name: idempotent matrix
concepts.description: A matrix A satisfying A² = A, characteristic of projection operators in linear algebra.
concepts.quote: Hξ Hξ = Hξ (idempotent), while Sλ Sλ ⪯ Sλ, meaning that the right-hand side exceeds the left-hand side by a positive semidefinite matrix.

concepts.name: positive semidefinite matrix
concepts.description: A symmetric matrix with non-negative eigenvalues, ensuring non-negative quadratic forms.
concepts.quote: Both are symmetric, positive semidefinite matrices.

concepts.name: eigendecomposition
concepts.description: The decomposition of a matrix into eigenvectors and eigenvalues, revealing the structure of linear operators.
concepts.quote: Since Sλ is symmetric (and positive semidefinite), it has a real eigendecomposition.

concepts.name: MARS
concepts.description: Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines - a greedy algorithm for adaptive knot placement in regression splines.
concepts.quote: The MARS procedure in Chapter 9 uses a greedy algorithm with some additional approximations to achieve a practical compromise.
